action.reasoning: Observation: I now have the full get_spherical_cameras source. The camera convention is: cam_pos=(dist*cos(el)*cos(az), dist*cos(el)*sin(az), dist*sin(el)), lookat=normalize(center-cam_pos), right=normalize(cross(lookat,up)), up=normalize(cross(right,lookat)), c2w=[right|up|-lookat|cam_pos]. My current predict.py is truncated and has issues. Analysis: I need to write a complete predict.py that exactly replicates TripoSR's camera convention for custom per-view camera poses. Decision: Write complete predict.py with a custom get_custom_cameras function that mirrors get_spherical_cameras but accepts per-view azimuth/elevation/distance, test on one validation object, then submit for evaluation.
